channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitics Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Pakistan PM confirms US-Iran peace deal text finalized
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that Washington and Tehran have reached a final agreed text for a peace deal, with both sides working on next steps toward implementation.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia trade drone strikes on energy infrastructure
item.description: Both sides exchanged overnight drone attacks, with Ukraine striking oil refineries and a petrochemicals plant while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV concludes Spain visit amid Catholic revival hopes
item.description: The pontiff's trip to Spain has spurred expectations among Catholic leaders of a spiritual revival in an increasingly secular nation.

item.title: Latin American markets rally on US-Iran peace deal prospects
item.description: Latin American assets rose as markets welcomed progress toward an agreement ending the US-Iran conflict, boosting global risk appetite.

item.title: EU Approves First Phase of Membership Talks for Ukraine and Moldova
item.description: EU ambassadors agreed to advance membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, with the first phase set to begin Monday. A significant step toward European integration for both nations.

item.title: Israeli-Palestinian Civil Society Groups Appeal for Two-State Solution in France
item.description: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups delivered an appeal in France urging the international community not to abandon a two-state solution as Paris seeks to keep the issue alive amid regional conflict.

item.title: How the EU Can Respond to the Second China Shock
item.description: European leaders face mounting pressure to address China's massive trade surplus and industrial policies. Reuters analyzes three distinct policy challenges requiring different EU responses at next week's summit.

item.title: Brazil Refiners Linked to Scheme Involving US-Designated Terrorist Group
item.description: Brazilian refineries sold over 100 million liters of naphtha to a firm investigated in an alleged fraud scheme involving a criminal gang designated by the US as a terrorist organization.

item.title: Anthropic Claude Fable 5 restrictions create new hurdles for China AI labs
item.description: Access restrictions on Anthropic's powerful model, reportedly targeted at Chinese AI labs, are sparking debate about safety versus innovation in the global AI race.

item.title: Iran hawks circle as emerging US-Iran deal details draw scrutiny
item.description: Iran hawks in Washington are already circling as details emerge about a potential agreement that Trump said could be signed as early as this weekend. Vice President Vance stated Iran will get no cash from any deal.

item.title: UAE expedites South Korean Cheongung II missile defense delivery
item.description: The UAE sent C-17 transport aircraft to South Korea to expedite delivery of the third M-SAM II (Cheongung II) battery, highlighting Gulf states' defensive preparations amid regional tensions.

item.title: US forces shoot down Iranian drones near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones as Tehran appeared to attempt to strike commercial ships transiting the strategic waterway, amid ongoing tensions and diplomatic talks.

item.title: UK judge links Palestine Action convictions to terrorism connection
item.description: A UK judge ruled that activists convicted over a raid on an Elbit Systems UK site will be sentenced on the basis of a terrorism connection in a landmark ruling with implications for protest-related cases.

item.title: US plans major cut to fighter jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
item.description: The United States plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe, the New York Times reported, citing two senior European officials. The move comes as the Trump administration shifts focus away from European defense.

item.title: Palestine Action activists to be sentenced as terrorists over UK raid on Israel defense firm
item.description: Four activists convicted of criminal damage at an Elbit Systems factory near Bristol will be sentenced as terrorists in what is believed to be a legal first in the UK. The ruling means they will not qualify for early release provisions.

item.title: Wall Street confronts newly led Federal Reserve as rate hike fears rattle markets
item.description: A suddenly rocky U.S. stock market confronts a potential wildcard next week: A newly led Federal Reserve at a time investors are worried that interest rate hikes to fight inflation could dampen enthusiasm for equities.

item.title: SpaceX Makes Historic Stock Market Debut After Record IPO
item.description: SpaceX began trading on Nasdaq after the largest initial public offering in history, with shares opening at $150 and Elon Musk becoming the world's first trillionaire as investors backed his vision spanning rockets and orbital AI.

item.title: Hezbollah Expects Lebanon Inclusion in Any US-Iran Deal
item.description: A leading Hezbollah politician expressed confidence that Iran will insist on Lebanon being included in any ceasefire agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a diplomatic resolution to the Gulf conflict.

item.title: UAE Stock Markets Hit Two-Month High on Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Stock markets in the United Arab Emirates surged Friday as regional hopes grew for a potential ceasefire deal between Iran and the United States that could end months of conflict in the Gulf.

item.title: Trump Denies Making Major Concessions to Iran, Says Leaked Deal Terms Are Untrue
item.description: President Trump pushed back against reports that the US had made significant concessions to Iran in peace negotiations, with a senior official calling any emerging pact performance-based with Tehran receiving no frozen assets until it fulfills its commitments.

item.title: Ukraine Air Force Warns of High-Probability Russian Missile Launch
item.description: Ukraine's Air Force warned of a high probability of a Russian missile launch from the Oreshnik site within the next day, expected to come from the Kapustin Yar launch site in southern Russia.

item.title: China-Russia Border River Tests Beijing's Ties With North Korea
item.description: A tiny river on Russia's border is testing China's relationship with Kim Jong-un, as Beijing seeks Sea of Japan access that neither China nor Pyongyang have publicly acknowledged.

item.title: Ukraine's Defense AI Chief Predicts 'New Paradigm' of Warfare
item.description: Ukraine's defense AI chief says the system that possesses more data and better understands it will gain advantage, predicting a fundamental shift in how warfare is conducted.

item.title: Iraq foils plot to assassinate national security service chief
item.description: Iraqi security forces thwarted an assassination plot targeting the head of the national security service and other senior officers, amid ongoing regional tensions.
